top_concerns.concern: Spain may manage qualification risk at 4 points, reducing both win rate and big-win tail.
top_concerns.evidence: Round-2 Spain 4-0 Saudi solved round-1 low-block issues, but third-round incentive and rotation risk now cut the other way.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: A strong-favorite Spain line can be too high if rotation, pace control, or draw-management is underweighted.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Confirm projected lineup intensity, yellow-card risk, and whether the model treats draw as sufficient for group control.

top_concerns.concern: Uruguay two-draw pressure can create late-game opening, but must-win should not be treated as a static kickoff state.
top_concerns.evidence: Uruguay drew Saudi 1-1 and Cape Verde 2-2; same-time dependency can change its risk profile during the match.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: If the model assumes full urgency from minute 1, it can overstate both early tempo and away-side variance.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Stress test first-half caution versus second-half opening scripts under different live table states.

top_concerns.concern: Cape Verde resilience is now evidence-backed after draws with Spain and Uruguay.
top_concerns.evidence: Cape Verde posted 0-0 versus Spain and 2-2 versus Uruguay, including repeat low-score survival and set-piece relevance.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Any line that treats Cape Verde as a soft favorite without draw and set-piece variance is too confident.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Keep draw tail and set-piece threat visible in both xG narrative and score matrix.

top_concerns.concern: Saudi Arabia must-win urgency increases both early-collapse and late-chaos failure modes.
top_concerns.evidence: Spain scored four against Saudi in round 2 after breaking the line early.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Saudi should not be modeled only as a low-output underdog; its volatility tails are wider now.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Split early defensive fragility from late-game transition chaos in scenario testing.

top_concerns.concern: China Sports Lottery pricing is missing, so market reaction to third-round motivation cannot be verified.
top_concerns.evidence: Collector file still has odds_snapshot_pending and no executable same-day market snapshot.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Without a traceable market snapshot, market alignment claims are weaker and betting language should remain blocked.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Add same-book 1X2 and handicap snapshot with capture time before any stronger publish language.

top_concerns.concern: The entire third-round quant chain is still incomplete.
top_concerns.evidence: Collector, tactics, and modeler files all remain in_progress skeletons with missing fields.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: This review can only be partial and interval-based, not a full audit of final probabilities.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Do not present exact final probabilities as fully validated until the quant chain exists.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: Spain rotates or manages risk after an even first half, leaving too much draw weight unmodeled.
Uruguay stays measured early, then opens sharply once live scoreboard pressure increases.
Cape Verde gets overpriced because win-and-qualify framing suppresses draw risk.
Saudi concedes early and the match flips into a high-variance state instead of a controlled Cape Verde script.
Same-time scoreboard changes alter late-game incentives in both matches.
overconfidence_flags: Revise if Spain win probability is above 0.41.
Revise if Uruguay win probability is below 0.27.
Revise if Cape Verde win probability is above 0.42.
Revise if Saudi win tail is pushed below 0.26.
Revise if any scoreline language implies high confidence without official T-75 lineups.
market_flags: No China Sports Lottery same-day snapshot is present, so third-round motivation pricing cannot be verified.
Do not use market agreement as proof while collector odds remain pending.
Keep betting language blocked until same-book executable prices are captured.
quant_chain_flags: data-collector.json is still skeleton-level in_progress with empty standings, fixtures, and odds arrays.
tactics-coach.json still has estimated lineups and missing official T-75 confirmation.
modeler-draft.json is still skeleton-level in_progress with no Poisson or group simulation outputs.
No final third-round quant output exists for H group.

spain_vs_uruguay.direction: Trim Spain favorite strength and big-win tail; keep draw and Uruguay late-open variance alive.

cape_verde_vs_saudi_arabia.direction: Do not overheat Cape Verde on pure momentum; preserve Saudi volatility and late-chaos tail.

handoff_notes: data: add current table, qualification paths, same-time dependency notes, and executable market snapshot.
tactics: review Spain rotation and pace management, Uruguay late opening triggers, Cape Verde set-piece resilience, and Saudi instability split.
model: avoid static motivation from kickoff, cap Spain favorite premium, and preserve draw plus volatility tails.
main: publish only as partial discussion output, not as full quant consensus.
